reforms.description: The New Széchenyi Venture Capital Programme was established to provide investments in high-technology young firms, significantly increasing venture capital investments in Hungary. Additionally, authorities plan to shift EU funds from infrastructure projects towards financial support for SMEs and innovation, aiming to better target financing for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The shift in funding and the establishment of the venture capital programme are expected to enhance the support for innovative firms, thereby fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The New Széchenyi Venture Capital Programme has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a significant venture capital programme and the strategic shift in funding represent major reforms in supporting innovation and SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities plan to shift funds from infrastructure projects towards financial support to SMEs, innovation and active labour market policies.

reforms.description: An important reform of vocational education and training (VET) entered into force in 2013, aiming to bring students’ skills closer to labour market needs by increasing the practical component of training and involving private companies more.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning vocational training with market needs, this reform is expected to enhance the skill set of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the vocational education landscape, making it a major reform in human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: An important reform of vocational education and training (VET) entered into force in 2013, which aims to bring students’ skills closer to labour market needs.
